programmes.show_description: Love Bites: Engaged couple Christopher and Tess inherited a New York diner, but working together has created a toxic work environment. Can Gordon help?
Weekend at Burnsie's: Dr Hibbert puts Homer on medicinal marijuana after he's attacked by a murder of crows.
Highway to Well: When Marge gets a job at a cannabis dispensary, she discovers a talent for healing, and Homer discovers a talent for dealing.
Thanksgiving of Horror: Tales of terror! A virtual Marge cooks against real Marge for survival. A murderous side dish threatens a space mission.
Selma's Choice: After Homer gets sick from a giant sandwich he's been eating for months, Selma steps in to take the kids to a new alcohol-based theme park.
Kirstie Allsopp shows us how to create beautiful crafts with items we already own. Inspired by nature, she takes to the garden to create some tiny terrariums and a hotel for bees and bugs.
High-flyers Bob and Louise, youngsters Will and Kim, and Iranian ex-pats Pasha and Haleh compete in Cheltenham, in a week of massive portions, a tale of heroism and Persian rice pudding.
Kirstie and Phil help two couples who are hoping to relocate to Surrey, one of the most expensive counties in the country, from a forever family home to a total lifestyle change.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Kelvin Fletcher in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Baby Balk: Rebecca and Sam decide to have a baby together, but when she tells him she wants the conception to be special, it puts him off. They each seek the Cranes' advice.
Get Your Kicks on Route 666: Frasier takes the guys on a road trip to rediscover his 'inner man', but things don't go to plan. Carla hires a relative to help out at the bar.
Madame LaCarla: Carla's psychic adviser decides to retire and asks Carla to take over her business, but Carla worries that she doesn't have 'the gift'.
Tissues: When Debra grudgingly agrees to let Ray be more involved in the daily decision-making, he starts shopping for things that he wants.
Snow Day: Stuck at Frank and Marie's during a snowstorm, Debra, Ray and Amy find themselves having a great time - until Debra makes a nasty comment towards Frank.
Cookies: Ray learns that the Frontier Girls' troupe leader and her daughter have targeted his parents' house as a spot to make big sales in their cookie drive.
Death and the Dog: On a quiet day at the radio station, Frasier tells a depressed caller about Eddie's recent run-in with the dog therapist.
Four for the Seesaw: Frasier and Niles invite two women on a romantic weekend vacation but become confused about their dates' intentions.
To Kill a Talking Bird: When Niles moves into a prestigious new building that doesn't allow cats or dogs, he buys himself a talking bird that ends up causing more problems.
Roz's Krantz & Gouldenstein Are Dead: Roz serves a community service order at a retirement home, but all the people who she looks after pass away.
Grudge Match: It's Frank versus Mike as the guys take to the track for a grudge match drag race to find out who has the fastest ride.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
Fiat Uno Turbo: In Wellingborough, Tim and Fuzz fix up one of the fastest hatchbacks of the 80s. Owner Gerry's ill health has meant that his Uno has been left to rot on his drive.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Windsor, Henry Cole finds an Austrian moped that's missing just one key item: an engine. And Simon O'Brien's taken with a fibreglass suit of armour called Ralph.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Laura Hamilton helps sisters Sheila and Jean to look for a holiday home on Spain's Mijas Costa for all their family and friends to enjoy, for £120k. Will they find their dream place?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren plan a disco revolution. In France, will juggling a working business and renovations prove too much for restaurateurs Andrew and Richard?
The new run continues. Fiona and Michael kick off this week's competition at their homely country pub in Norfolk. But a problem with a shower makes a guest feel dour.
The new run continues. In South Wales, farm girl Claire treats her guests to a Welsh spread and farm-themed games. Will her farm charm impress or will her night end up a mucky mess? (Ep1/5)
Leah Charles-King helps Sarah and Andy find the 'pawfect' place for their whole family - including their beloved dog Ruby - on Spain's southern Costa Blanca, for their budget of £160k.
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.
Cooking for Less: Can Joe and Suzanne transform chaotic cooking into delightful dinners for the Burton family, including for two children with coeliac disease?
...Standing: Could you survive being handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? In a new TV experiment, Jonathan Ross challenges 18 Brits to stay together, as they compete to win a £100k prize. (Ep1)
In the centre of Brighton, a lone officer is violently attacked and urgently needs back-up. And a disorientated drunk going home to bed tries to smash his way into the wrong house.
Brighton's night police patrol the town's hectic night life - from a possible suicide up on the cliff, to an urgent manhunt for a violent assailant with a crowbar.
Air ambulances bring the emergency room to critically ill or injured people across the country, including a man in cardiac arrest and a woman who's been trampled by a foal.
In an idyllic Oxfordshire hamlet, the fish in the river keep dying. Why? David Thewlis and Jason Watkins lead in this real-life drama of victims, whistleblowers and water companies. (Ep1)
The drama continues as David Thewlis stars in a real-life story of pollution and criminal convictions that exposes how water companies have polluted our natural resources for so long. (Ep2)
The drama concludes as David Thewlis leads in a real-life story of pollution and criminal convictions that exposes how water companies have polluted our natural resources for so long. (Ep3)

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Brilliant Builds: Kirstie and Phil look back at lofty ambitions in Walthamstow in East London, where the Clayton-Fisher family totally transformed their dark and dingy attic.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Professional luthier Gary helps Simon O'Brien to restore an old bluegrass banjo.
Rex continues to explore his identity as secrets threaten to spill at the joint engagement party. Gemma faces a painful revelation. And Mercedes returns from rehab with a shocking proposal.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
The Norm Who Came in from the Cold: Norm hurts his back while painting Frasier and Lilith's apartment so Frasier lets him sleep on the sofa. Rebecca tests her mothering skills.
Ma's Little Maggie: Cliff's worried about his girlfriend meeting his mum, but they get along famously. Rebecca aims to improve Sam's fertility.
Unplanned Parenthood: Rebecca and Sam argue over what last name their future child should have, and look after Carla's unruly kids for a night.
Lucky Suit: Robert's ecstatic when he's recommended for an FBI job, until it becomes apparent that Marie's meddling may scupper his chances.
The Skit: At a party, Ray and Debra do an amusing impersonation of Frank and Marie. But when the older couple turn the tables on them, Ray and Debra don't find it so funny.
The Breakup Tape: When Debra finds a cassette that contains a message from Ray's college girlfriend, she wants to know why he's kept it for so many years.
The Unnatural: Frasier tries to stop Frederick from realising that he's a less-than-perfect father when he fills in on the radio station's baseball team.
Roz's Turn: When a spot opens up at the radio station for a new talk show host, Roz decides that it's time for her to take the mic.
Ham Radio: Frasier's attempt to produce a live, 1940s-style radio drama for KACL's 50th anniversary proves disastrous when Niles takes matters into his own hands.
Three Dates and a Breakup (Part 1/2): Frasier has dates with three very different women over one weekend. Meanwhile, Martin and Sherry consider splitting up.
Escape to Motor Mountain: The guys find a mountain of motorcycles, but can they get through the avalanche of booby traps? Plus: a stubborn collector and a warehouse mega-pick.
Channel 4's midday news summary.
VW Corrado: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue Ashley's 1992 VW Corrado, which has been rotting away since he fell ill. Can the boys bring some joy back to Ashley's life?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Crewe, Simon O'Brien and Max McMurdo meet rock 'n' roller Billy, who lives in a house that's frozen in time in the 1950s.
Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Neil Delamere in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.
Sarah and Ian met in Spain and now want a holiday home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great finds.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: On the Costa Blanca, Abi and Darren's 'boogie wonderland' is hit by technical trouble. And Gordon and Shona finally take the reins of a diving business in Marbella.
The new run continues. Carl and Liz host the week's second visit, at their coaching inn in Cambridgeshire, where converted stables and Highland cows are on the menu (not literally).
The new run continues. In South Wales, Richard hosts at his pub, setting the bar high with terrific tartlets, sensational seabass and crowd-pleasing cheesecake. Can he hit bullseye? (Ep2/5)
Antonia and Ross love Ibiza. They've holidayed there many times, and now hope to find their own holiday home there. Can Lucy Alexander deliver their dream?
Channel 4's flagship news programme. Including the weather.

programmes.show_name: New: Nando's vs KFC: Battle of...
programmes.show_description: ...the Brands: KFC and Nando's go head-to-head as Kate Quilton explores the battle for our taste buds in a fiery look at flavour, fandom and a billion-pound fight to win the chicken wars.
...Standing: Could you stay handcuffed to a stranger 24/7? Self-confessed prude Charlie is hooked to heavily tattooed Rob. Can Christian hairdresser Nina handle Sara's chatty nature? (Ep2)
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
This time it's Corrie, The Stolen Girl, The Piano, Mickey Rourke on Celebrity Big Brother, savoury cocktails on Saturday Kitchen, the News on Katy Perry in space, and more.
